items.body: Links golf doesn't care who's world No. 1 — and that's the fun part. Scheffler tops a loaded board but he's still hunting win No. 2 this year, and Renaissance has been weird on him before. McIlroy's last three trips here: first, fourth, second — he knows this stretch of coast cold. Rahm's back in a PGA Tour field for the first time since LIV, Gotterup just won the John Deere with a Sunday 62 and now defends here, and with Royal Birkdale waiting next week, this is the best preview you'll get of who's actually ready for The Open.

items.label: Scottie Scheffler (+400 to +500):
items.body: The clear betting favorite and world No. 1, still hunting his first win of 2026 after a strong run all season.

items.label: Rory McIlroy (+900 to +1000):
items.body: The 2023 champion who knows links golf as well as anyone and always raises his game in Scotland.

items.label: Jon Rahm (+1100 to +1200):
items.body: A major champion making a rare PGA Tour appearance this week thanks to the event's DP World Tour co-sanction.

items.label: Matt Fitzpatrick (+1900 to +2200):
items.body: An elite links player and former U.S. Amateur champion at nearby Royal Lytham who thrives in windy conditions.

items.label: Xander Schauffele (+2000 to +2200):
items.body: The 2022 winner here and a proven big-stage performer on coastal layouts.

items.label: Tommy Fleetwood (+2100 to +2500):
items.body: A fan favorite whose creative shot-making and links pedigree make him dangerous every time the Tour hits the coast.

items.label: Ludvig Aberg (+2200 to +2600):
items.body: A rising star with the ball-striking to handle firm fairways and fast greens.

items.label: Chris Gotterup (+3200 to +3500):
items.body: The defending champion riding a hot streak with four PGA Tour wins already this season.

items.label: Viktor Hovland (+3200 to +3600):
items.body: A major winner whose power and iron play can overpower a links test when conditions soften.

items.label: Robert MacIntyre (+3800 to +4200):
items.body: The 2024 hometown hero who became the first Scot to win his national open in a generation — the crowd will be with him.

items.label: Venue:
items.body: The Renaissance Club in North Berwick, East Lothian, Scotland, along the Firth of Forth on Scotland's Golf Coast.

items.label: Established:
items.body: The Scottish Open dates to 1972 and has been co-sanctioned by the PGA Tour and DP World Tour since 2022; The Renaissance Club has hosted since 2019.

items.label: Defending Champion:
items.body: Chris Gotterup won the 2025 edition at 15-under, his second career PGA Tour title.

items.label: Tradition:
items.body: Scotland's national open and a DP World Tour Rolex Series event; the top three non-exempt finishers earn spots in The Open Championship.

items.label: Course:
items.body: The Renaissance Club, North Berwick, Scotland.

items.label: Dates:
items.body: July 9 through July 12, 2026.

items.label: Purse:
items.body: $9 million.

items.label: Format:
items.body: 156-player field (PGA Tour and DP World Tour), 72-hole stroke play.

items.label: Course Profile:
items.body: A Tom Doak-designed 7,282-yard par-70 links course with firm fescue fairways, pot bunkers, and coastal winds that reward creative shot-making.

items.label: Coverage:
items.body: Thursday and Friday on Golf Channel (11 a.m.-2 p.m. ET); weekend rounds on Golf Channel (10 a.m.-noon ET) and CBS (noon-3 p.m. ET), with streaming on Paramount+ and PGA TOUR LIVE on ESPN+.

items.label: Event Window:
items.body: Competition runs July 9 through July 12, 2026.
